title: Level 343
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 343: "Quartered Ruins"}} {{Quote|I feel like someone lives here, however I don't see a single soul in sight. The ruined home seems somewhat comforting, yet sinister at the same time. |An anonymous traveler, found on a small piece of paper}} {{Class 3e}} {{Background|file=lvl343h1.jpg}} '''Level 343''' is the 344th level of The Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=lvl343h1.jpg|hovertext=ruined.|caption=A photo of '''Level 343'''.}} == Description == '''Level 343''', otherwise known as '''Quartered Ruins''', appears to be an endlessly stretching ruin of a building, presumably a living quarter from the 1960s. The level's walls are destroyed or cracked while built from a solid brick surface. One of the only structures in usable condition are pillars rising from the ground to the ceiling, having identical characteristics to a wall. The floor is in similar circumstances, being painted in a bland gray color, and its height is frequently uneven. The ceiling is located very high, approximately 15 feet above the ground, and has a darker shade of brown splattered onto it with concrete showing at some spots that aren't painted. '''Level 343''' has proven itself to be challenging to navigate, even for experienced explorers. The level is filled with trash, most commonly building materials like a metallic nail or a piece of concrete. These items are hardly ever in operable condition, with most being actively harmful to anyone who explores the level. Scarcely, the floor can crack, revealing an opening into [[The Void]]. While the level is well-lit, it does frequently possess dim sections, so having a light source is necessary. '''Level 343's''' structuring is perplexing. A lot of the walls and corridors seem completely unethical and unreasonable in their placement, along with numerous unique anomalies in this level. Hallways can abruptly end with no way ahead, which forces one to backtrack. The ceiling can occasionally have cracks, revealing a light, which is most likely artificial sunlight. The pillars can defy the laws of gravity; they can stay afloat between the floor and the ceiling, they can have completely nonsensical supports, yet they stay upright. These qualities make the level incredibly hard to map. There is a possibility of the level behaving normally, like an actual ruined building, however, the chances of this event happening are slim. When it occurs, the level seemingly fixes its physically impossible features - the pillars go back to being geometrically correct, there isn't [[The Void|a void]] under the floor, the hallways behave like hallways one would find in [[The Frontrooms]]. If this event happens, the level becomes much less hostile to explore, however after a while, it will morph back into its original, mangled state. {{Blurimage|file=lvl343h2.jpg|hovertext=ruined.|caption=Another image of '''Level 343'''.}} '''Level 343''' seems to be in a state of isolation. It seems likely for a now-extinct society to have resided here in the distant past, becuase the level's components point to being a ruined living quarter from the 1960s, and there have been reports of marks and graffiti by anonymous authors. Nonetheless, there have been no traces of a potential society that could have lived in '''Level 343'''. Any evolution that the level can have is fully negated, as time seems to be practically nonexistent, estimated to stay at noon, and any decay that the level was endangered to cannot happen, as the existence of time is necessary for any decomposition to take place. If a predetermined group of 2 people or more is set to enter '''Level 343''', it is guaranteed that each member of that group will be sent at a completely obsolete location from the others, presumably a minimum of 50 kilometers apart. It is unknown how big the level truly is, and while there have been attempts to reach the conclusive end of '''Level 343''', those attempts were unsuccessful. == Anomalies == Due to its unsafe and abandoned nature, '''Level 343''' is completely devoid of any life. It is unknown why this is the case, as most entities that reside in The Backrooms would be able to remain at this level for prolonged chunks of time. Nonetheless, numerous unique environmental hazards can occur in '''Level 343'''. There is a possibility that not all of the anomalies have been witnessed; the number of threats that exist at this level is undisclosable. These events possess incredibly mysterious properties; there is no reasonable explanation as to why these dangers have being in this level. === The Darkened === {{Blurimage|file=thedarkened343.jpg|hovertext=ruined.|caption=An image of '''The Darkened'''. The photo seems to be taken from a small crack in a wall.}} '''The Darkened''' is an anomaly in which the level will become shrouded in darkness. The sunlight from the cracked ceiling suddenly disappears, leaving the level with a complete lack of visibility. If one possesses a light source, it will malfunction in a way that makes it completely unviable and irreparable. The light source's structure and appearance stay the same as it appeared before, but it is rumored that the core components shatter into microscopically small pieces. There's no known range of time that '''The Darkened''' can linger for. The shortest duration of this hazard has been documented to be around 20 minutes, and the longest known duration is approximated to be 2-3 weeks. Unfortunately, this event makes it practically impossible to explore the level without causing self-harm. The best action to do when witnessing '''The Darkened''' is to find a safe spot and avoid any major foot movements, due to scattered trash mentioned in the description. '''The Darkened''' may cause the level to corrupt itself, however it's not guaranteed due to the conditions of the event making it very hard to uncover all of the information about it. It possesses a lot of unexplainable properties, and in some cases, inconsistencies. There has been cases of travelers finding a fully functional light source during this event. Nonetheless, this is meant to be taken with a grain of salt, as a lot of these documentations can be red herrings. === The Cracked === '''The Cracked''' is much more dangerous than '''The Darkened''', however it occurs a lot less frequently. The event causes the floor to gradually crack, eventually completely collapsing, which reveals [[The Void]] under it. The disappearance of the floor happens over a very short time, approximately 10-15 seconds. After the ground is fully wiped out of existence, it will phase back into its place after approximately half an hour in an undisclosed way. The only hope one has to survive during this event occurs is holding onto a broken pillar or a short enough wall to grab onto it, however, due to the amount of time one has to hold on to a surface without slipping, it's been estimated that '''The Cracked''' is unfeasible to survive. The form that the ground takes after reincarnating a new version itself after cracking seems more reinforced, with fewer cracks being present and having less of a height inconsistency, keeping at a fairly stable level of elevation. The level seemingly becomes more stable after '''The Cracked'''. == Colonies & Outposts == Due to the isolated and separating nature of '''Level 343''', forming a community is impossible. == Entrances And Exits == === Entrances === * Going through an empty doorframe seemingly leading to nowhere in [[Level 560]] will take you here. * Finding a door labeled "MAGAZINE" in the convenience store located on [[Level 711]] will create a direct entrance to here. * Noclipping through a tree on the path to the shoreline in [[Level 215]] may take one here. === Exits === * The easiest way to exit the level is by falling through one of the pitfalls created in the floor, sending one to [[The Void]], but for obvious reasons, it's heavily advised to not exit through this path. * If one discovers an oddly colored wall compared to the other ones, noclipping through it will take one to [[Level 111]]. * Certain hallways may fade into section 1 of [[Level 311]].
